hook: Capital is moving — here's where Singapore stands right now

slides.sub: Fed paralysis, USD softness, and regional capital flight — what it means for Singapore property buyers right now.

slides.lead: Three macro forces collided this week — and Singapore is at the centre.
slides.source: MAS · Fed FOMC · Bloomberg · POV Guy analysis

slides.pre: USD/SGD spot rate, week of May 3
slides.post: SGD near 12-month high vs USD — historically correlated with inbound capital interest in S'pore real assets.

slides.title: Three forces driving capital toward Singapore

items.body: Fed paralysis keeps US rate uncertainty alive — USD-denominated holdings lose relative yield appeal vs SGD assets.
Regional risk-off (Middle East + Taiwan Strait noise) historically redirects UHNWI liquidity into SGD-pegged hard assets.
MAS has not followed the Fed lower — positive SGD real yield differential makes S'pore property a carry-positive store of value.

slides.source: MAS · Fed FOMC · POV Guy analysis

slides.title: Singapore property vs global alternatives

items.label: London Prime
items.sub: GBP vol + UK fiscal drag weigh on appeal

items.label: Dubai Freehold
items.sub: High beta to oil & Gulf risk sentiment

slides.source: POV Guy analysis · Knight Frank · URA

slides.label: The real signal
slides.punch: When the Fed stalls and the USD softens simultaneously, UHNWI capital doesn't sit in cash — it moves into hard assets in stable jurisdictions. Singapore ticks every box: rule of law, SGD resilience, transparent title, and a government that has never defaulted on property rights.
slides.punch_bold: The geopolitical premium in CCR is not priced in yet. That window is narrow.

slides.pre: CCR non-landed transactions, Q1 2026 vs Q1 2025
slides.post: Foreign-profile buyers led the uptick — consistent with capital-flight inflows during prior risk-off cycles (2015, 2020, 2022).
slides.source: URA caveats · POV Guy analysis · approx. Q1 rolling

caption:
📉 Fed stalls, USD softens — and Singapore property quietly becomes the global safe-haven trade.

• USD/SGD near ~1.34, a 12-month SGD high — historically a leading inflow signal for S'pore real assets.
• CCR non-landed volumes up ~18% YoY in Q1 2026, led by foreign-profile buyers in risk-off mode.
• MAS holds its line — positive SGD real yield differential makes S'pore property carry-positive vs cash.

When the world gets nervous, capital moves to stable jurisdictions with transparent title. Singapore wins that trade every time — and the CCR geopolitical premium isn't fully priced yet.
